setup:
Hundred Grid Mode B companion display shows the canonical 4 × 5 = 20 rectangle (build affordances disabled). §2.11 Multiple Choice / Checkbox surfaces four equation candidates. Tutor prompt (student-facing): "Look at this rectangle. Choose ALL the equations that correctly describe it." (Both correct equations must be selected for a green check.)

Candidate set: `4 × 5 = 20` ✓, `20 = 4 × 5` ✓, `4 + 5 = 20` ✗ (addition distractor), `4 × 20 = 5` ✗ (product/side-length swap distractor).

Post-Check Drop Down stem: `These equations are correct because ___.` Pre-filled options: "they show two ways to write the same fact" / "the equal sign means both sides have the same value" / "one of them must be wrong." Both first two options are reinforcing; student selects one (either accepted).
student_action: Student selects equations via checkbox taps, then taps Check. Post-Check: student completes the Drop Down stem.
teacher_move: If both correct options are selected: "Both of those describe the same rectangle — two ways to write the same fact." If only one correct option is selected: Light-tier intervention (pulse the unselected panel in Mode C; prompt "Is there another equation that describes this same rectangle?"). If an incorrect option is also selected: "Look at that equation. Does `4 + 5` equal 20? What operation does this rectangle show?"
key_observation: Student selects BOTH `4 × 5 = 20` AND `20 = 4 × 5` — bidirectional recognition of reversed orientations as both correct. This is the primary observable for L.2.
common_detour: Student selects only `4 × 5 = 20` — U1.6 pattern. Tutor invokes Light-tier (Mode C right-panel pulse). If pattern persists: Medium-tier (balance scale re-invocation).
intervention: Light → Medium per §1.4.1 remediation ladder. Use the same canonical 4 × 5 = 20 rectangle — no new rectangle.
